title_en: Sunni Engagement with Al-Kashaf: Appropriation and Critique
content_en:
The history of Sunni scholars' engagement with Al-Kashaf is a study in how the Islamic scholarly tradition manages complex texts — works of genuine value produced from within a heterodox theological framework. The tradition did not simply accept or reject Al-Kashaf; it developed a sophisticated methodology for using it selectively and critically, and this methodology itself became a part of the scholarly heritage.

The earliest major Sunni engagement was that of al-Fakhr al-Razi (544–606 AH / 1149–1210 CE), whose encyclopedic commentary Mafatih al-Ghayb (also known as Tafsir al-Kabir or Tafsir al-Razi) frequently borrows al-Zamakhshari's linguistic insights while arguing against his theological positions. Al-Razi's commentary is arguably the most comprehensive tafsir ever produced, and its engagement with Al-Kashaf — agreeing where al-Zamakhshari's grammar is sound, disagreeing where his theology deviates from Ash'ari Sunni positions — set the template for how later scholars would approach the work.

Al-Baydawi (d. 685 AH / 1286 CE) produced Anwar al-Tanzil wa-Asrar al-Ta'wil, which many scholars consider the most successful 'purified' adaptation of Al-Kashaf. Al-Baydawi took al-Zamakhshari's linguistic analyses, stripped them of their Mu'tazili theological glosses, and replaced the heterodox elements with Ash'ari Sunni positions. The result is a commentary that preserves the linguistic depth of Al-Kashaf while being sound in its theology. Al-Baydawi's commentary became one of the most widely taught tafsirs in the Ottoman scholarly tradition and attracted dozens of supercommentaries (hashiyas) of its own. It is, in a sense, Al-Kashaf made safe for the Sunni madrasa — though advanced students were expected to read the original as well.

Abu Hayyan al-Andalusi (654–745 AH / 1256–1344 CE), the master of Quranic Arabic grammar from Spain, engaged Al-Kashaf extensively in his tafsir Al-Bahr al-Muhit (The Surrounding Ocean). Abu Hayyan frequently disagrees with al-Zamakhshari on grammatical as well as theological grounds — showing that even on the linguistic level, Al-Kashaf's readings are not above scholarly debate. His critique sharpened the tradition's understanding of where al-Zamakhshari's grammatical analysis is sound and where it is driven by his theological commitments.

In the Ottoman scholarly world, Al-Kashaf was a standard advanced text. Students who had mastered Al-Baydawi or Tafsir al-Jalalain were directed to Al-Kashaf for deeper linguistic study, under the guidance of teachers who could navigate its theological complexity. The Ottoman scholarly tradition produced numerous hawashi on Al-Kashaf, including by scholars like Mulla Jami and others, that provided theological commentary on al-Zamakhshari's positions.

This pattern of critical engagement represents the mature Sunni scholarly approach to Al-Kashaf: recognize its linguistic genius, acknowledge its theological deviations from Sunni consensus, study it with the critiques alongside it, and derive from it what is sound while setting aside what is not. This is not compromise — it is the application of the scholars' principle that wisdom is the believer's lost property, to be taken wherever it is found, while false doctrine is identified and refused.
title_id: Keterlibatan Sunni dengan Al-Kashaf: Apropriasi dan Kritik
content_id:
Sejarah keterlibatan ulama Sunni dengan Al-Kashaf adalah sebuah kajian tentang bagaimana tradisi keilmuan Islam mengelola teks-teks yang kompleks — karya-karya bernilai genuinely yang dihasilkan dari dalam kerangka teologis heterodoks. Tradisi ini tidak sekadar menerima atau menolak Al-Kashaf; ia mengembangkan metodologi canggih untuk menggunakannya secara selektif dan kritis, dan metodologi itu sendiri menjadi bagian dari warisan keilmuan.

Keterlibatan Sunni utama yang paling awal adalah yang dilakukan oleh al-Fakhr al-Razi (544–606 H / 1149–1210 M), yang komentarnya yang ensiklopedis Mafatih al-Ghayb (juga dikenal sebagai Tafsir al-Kabir atau Tafsir al-Razi) sering meminjam wawasan linguistik al-Zamakhshari sambil berargumen menentang posisi-posisi teologisnya. Komentar al-Razi adalah tafsir paling komprehensif yang pernah dibuat, dan keterlibatannya dengan Al-Kashaf — setuju di mana tata bahasa al-Zamakhshari benar, tidak setuju di mana teologinya menyimpang dari posisi Sunni Asy'ari — menetapkan template bagaimana ulama-ulama belakangan akan mendekati karya tersebut.

Al-Baydawi (w. 685 H / 1286 M) menghasilkan Anwar al-Tanzil wa-Asrar al-Ta'wil, yang oleh banyak ulama dianggap sebagai adaptasi 'yang telah dimurnikan' dari Al-Kashaf yang paling berhasil. Al-Baydawi mengambil analisis-analisis linguistik al-Zamakhshari, menanggalkan komentar-komentar teologis Mu'tazilahinya, dan mengganti unsur-unsur heterodoks dengan posisi-posisi Sunni Asy'ari. Hasilnya adalah sebuah komentar yang mempertahankan kedalaman linguistik Al-Kashaf sekaligus sehat dalam teologinya. Komentar al-Baydawi menjadi salah satu tafsir yang paling banyak diajarkan dalam tradisi keilmuan Ottoman dan menarik lusinan superkomenter (hasyiyah) tersendiri. Ia, dalam satu pengertian, merupakan Al-Kashaf yang telah dibuat aman untuk madrasah Sunni — meski para pelajar tingkat lanjut diharapkan membaca karya aslinya pula.

Abu Hayyan al-Andalusi (654–745 H / 1256–1344 M), maestro tata bahasa Arab Qurani dari Spanyol, terlibat secara ekstensif dengan Al-Kashaf dalam tafsirnya Al-Bahr al-Muhit (Samudra yang Meliputi). Abu Hayyan sering tidak sependapat dengan al-Zamakhshari dalam hal gramatikal maupun teologis — menunjukkan bahwa bahkan pada tataran linguistik, bacaan-bacaan Al-Kashaf tidak luput dari perdebatan keilmuan. Kritiknya mempertajam pemahaman tradisi tentang di mana analisis gramatikal al-Zamakhshari benar dan di mana ia didorong oleh komitmen teologisnya.

Dalam dunia keilmuan Ottoman, Al-Kashaf merupakan teks lanjutan yang standar. Para pelajar yang telah menguasai Al-Baydawi atau Tafsir al-Jalalain diarahkan ke Al-Kashaf untuk kajian linguistik yang lebih mendalam, di bawah bimbingan guru-guru yang mampu menavigasi kompleksitas teologisnya. Tradisi keilmuan Ottoman menghasilkan banyak hawasyi atas Al-Kashaf, termasuk oleh ulama-ulama seperti Mulla Jami dan lainnya, yang memberikan komentar teologis atas posisi-posisi al-Zamakhshari.

Pola keterlibatan kritis ini mencerminkan pendekatan keilmuan Sunni yang matang terhadap Al-Kashaf: akui kejeniusan linguistiknya, sadari penyimpangan-penyimpangan teologisnya dari konsensus Sunni, pelajari bersama kritik-kritiknya, dan ambil darinya apa yang sehat seraya mengesampingkan apa yang tidak. Ini bukan kompromi — ini adalah penerapan prinsip para ulama bahwa hikmah adalah harta yang hilang milik orang beriman, untuk diambil di mana pun ditemukan, sementara doktrin yang salah diidentifikasi dan ditolak.

The التراث did not simply accept or reject Al-Kashaf; it developed a sophisticated methodology for using it selectively and critically, and this methodology itself became a part of the العلمي heritage. Al-Razi's commentary is arguably the most comprehensive التفسير ever produced, and its engagement with Al-Kashaf — agreeing where al-Zamakhshari's grammar is sound, disagreeing where his theology deviates from الأشعري Sunni positions — set the template for how later العلماء would approach the work.
